ddls:
CREATE TABLE "network_devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the network device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of the network device (e.g., router, switch) */,
  "interface" TEXT /* Interface of the network device (e.g., Ethernet, Wi-Fi) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical or logical location of the device */,
  "vendor" TEXT /* Vendor of the device */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the device */,
  "firmware_version" TEXT /* Current firmware version of the device */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last time the device information was updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the device (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the metric belongs to */,
  "time_since_last_recording" REAL /* Time since the last recording in seconds */,
  "latency" REAL /* Current latency of the network */,
  "cost" REAL /* Current cost of the network */,
  "reliability" REAL /* Current reliability of the network */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the metric */,
  "bandwidth_usage" REAL /* Current bandwidth usage */,
  "packet_loss" REAL /* Percentage of packet loss */,
  "jitter" REAL /* Network jitter */,
  "uptime" INTEGER /* Uptime of the device in seconds */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the device */,
  "device_vendor" TEXT /* Vendor of the device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES network_devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the metric the prediction belongs to */,
  "predicted_latency" REAL /* Predicted latency of the network */,
  "predicted_cost" REAL /* Predicted cost of the network */,
  "predicted_reliability" REAL /* Predicted reliability of the network */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the prediction */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the machine learning model used for prediction */,
  "confidence_score" REAL /* Confidence score of the prediction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES machine_learning_models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES performance_metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ping_data" (
  "ping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ping data */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the ping data belongs to */,
  "time_since_last_ping" REAL /* Time since the last ping in seconds */,
  "ping_success" INTEGER /* Whether the ping was successful (1) or not (0) */,
  "ping_time" REAL /* Time taken for the ping */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the ping */,
  "packet_loss" INTEGER /* Number of packets lost during the ping */,
  "jitter" REAL /* Jitter of the ping */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the device */,
  "device_vendor" TEXT /* Vendor of the device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ping_data_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES network_devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "daywise_data" (
  "day_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each day-wise data */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the day-wise data belongs to */,
  "day1" INTEGER /* Value for day 1 */,
  "day2" INTEGER /* Value for day 2 */,
  "day3" INTEGER /* Value for day 3 */,
  "day4" INTEGER /* Value for day 4 */,
  "day5" INTEGER /* Value for day 5 */,
  "day6" INTEGER /* Value for day 6 */,
  "day7" INTEGER /* Value for day 7 */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the day-wise data */,
  "average_latency" REAL /* Average latency over the week */,
  "average_cost" REAL /* Average cost over the week */,
  "average_reliability" REAL /* Average reliability over the week */,
  "average_bandwidth_usage" REAL /* Average bandwidth usage over the week */,
  "average_packet_loss" REAL /* Average packet loss over the week */,
  "average_jitter" REAL /* Average jitter over the week */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("day_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_daywise_data_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES network_devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "expected_performance" (
  "expected_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each expected performance */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the metric the expected performance belongs to */,
  "expected_latency" REAL /* Expected latency of the network */,
  "expected_cost" REAL /* Expected cost of the network */,
  "expected_reliability" REAL /* Expected reliability of the network */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the expected performance */,
  "expected_bandwidth_usage" REAL /* Expected bandwidth usage */,
  "expected_packet_loss" REAL /* Expected packet loss */,
  "expected_jitter" REAL /* Expected jitter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("expected_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_expected_performance_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES performance_metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the alert belongs to */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the metric the alert belongs to */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., latency, cost, reliability) */,
  "threshold" REAL /* Threshold value for the alert */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the alert */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "alert_status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., active, resolved) */,
  "alert_description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "alert_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the alert in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES performance_metrics ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES network_devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last time the user logged in */,
  "last_activity" TEXT /* Last time the user was active */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the data belongs to */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the metric the data belongs to */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES performance_metrics ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES network_devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "machine_learning_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each machine learning model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the machine learning model */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of the machine learning model (e.g., regression, classification) */,
  "model_parameters" TEXT /* Parameters of the machine learning model */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the machine learning model */,
  "model_accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the model */,
  "model_training_date" TEXT /* Date when the model was trained */,
  "model_description" TEXT /* Description of the model */,
  "model_version" TEXT /* Version of the model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)
